opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Every time I walk up the slight hill to my house, I get this heavy squeezing in my chest that goes down my left arm, but it completely disappears if I stop and rest for five minutes.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Recurrent, predictable heavy chest pain when walking up the hill to the house, causing worry about a possible heart attack; my spouse is very anxious about it.

symptom_narrative.duration: each episode settles completely within 5-10 minutes of standing still to rest
symptom_narrative.character: heavy, squeezing tightness in the centre of the chest
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: uncomfortable enough that I now stop and rest before I reach the top of the hill to avoid it
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: central/retrosternal chest, spreading down the inner side of the left arm
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes on predictably at the same point every time - walking about 200 metres up the slight incline - and always eases with rest; the pattern has been the same for the past 4 weeks
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: stopping and resting for 5-10 minutes makes it go away completely
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: walking up the hill, physical exertion, and it is worse in cold weather
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a bit short of breath at exactly the same time as the chest pain when walking; no pain at all when I am resting
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: this has been happening the same way for about 4 weeks now
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: changing how I do things to avoid the exertion that brings it on, but still independent

past_medical_history.how_managed: on a daily blood pressure tablet

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse; house is on a steep incline, which is the daily trigger for walking up

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened these episodes are 'mini heart attacks'; spouse is highly anxious about it

communication_style.typical_message_style: describes the predictable exertional pattern clearly

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single self-reported home reading; cuff calibration unknown
